05_Final_script

Judy Hua Zhu, Trudy Jiang

2026-04-21

GitHub Repository link: “https://github.com/judyhuazhu/Jiang_Zhu_Trjudy_Team.git”

01 read all data

library(readxl)
library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.1.4     ✔ readr     2.1.5
## ✔ forcats   1.0.0     ✔ stringr   1.5.1
## ✔ ggplot2   3.5.1     ✔ tibble    3.2.1
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.3     ✔ tidyr     1.3.1
## ✔ purrr     1.0.2     
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(here)
## here() starts at /home/guest/TSA_ForecastCompetition_Jiang_Zhu_Trjudy_Team
read_excel(here("Data", "load.xlsx"))
## # A tibble: 2,372 × 26
##    meter_id date                   h1    h2    h3    h4    h5    h6    h7    h8
##    <chr>    <dttm>              <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>
##  1 0072     2005-01-01 00:00:00  3072  2911  2784  2768  2832  2904  3064  3192
##  2 0072     2005-01-02 00:00:00  2408  2328  2311  2328  2400  2552  2856  3272
##  3 0072     2005-01-03 00:00:00  2575  2528  2496  2608  2825  3376  3760  3672
##  4 0072     2005-01-04 00:00:00  2177  2016  1951  1913  2080  2624  3328  2896
##  5 0072     2005-01-05 00:00:00  1912  1768  1697  1720  1880  2472  3208  2888
##  6 0072     2005-01-06 00:00:00  2104  1976  1920  1952  2167  2736  3432  3096
##  7 0072     2005-01-07 00:00:00  2336  2296  2336  2448  2712  3408  4248  3943
##  8 0072     2005-01-08 00:00:00  2849  2695  2640  2600  2728  2984  3144  3432
##  9 0072     2005-01-09 00:00:00  3264  3199  3240  3336  3480  3641  4015  4568
## 10 0072     2005-01-10 00:00:00  3520  3568  3584  3728  3943  4680  5256  5000
## # ℹ 2,362 more rows
## # ℹ 16 more variables: h9 <dbl>, h10 <dbl>, h11 <dbl>, h12 <dbl>, h13 <dbl>,
## #   h14 <dbl>, h15 <dbl>, h16 <dbl>, h17 <dbl>, h18 <dbl>, h19 <dbl>,
## #   h20 <dbl>, h21 <dbl>, h22 <dbl>, h23 <dbl>, h24 <dbl>
load <- read_excel("Data/load.xlsx")
temp <- read_excel("Data/temperature.xlsx")
humidity <- read_excel("Data/relative_humidity.xlsx")
# check structure
str(load)
str(temp)
str(humidity)

# check first few rows
head(load)
head(temp)
head(humidity)
# process load to daily average
daily_load <- load %>%
  mutate(
    date = as.Date(date),
    daily_avg = rowMeans(select(., starts_with("h")), na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  select(date, daily_avg)

head(daily_load)
## # A tibble: 6 × 2
##   date       daily_avg
##   <date>         <dbl>
## 1 2005-01-01     3108.
## 2 2005-01-02     3068.
## 3 2005-01-03     3061.
## 4 2005-01-04     2566.
## 5 2005-01-05     2496.
## 6 2005-01-06     2735.
str(daily_load)
## tibble [2,372 × 2] (S3: tbl_df/tbl/data.frame)
##  $ date     : Date[1:2372], format: "2005-01-01" "2005-01-02" ...
##  $ daily_avg: num [1:2372] 3108 3068 3061 2566 2496 ...
# process temperature
daily_temp <- temp %>%
  mutate(
    date = as.Date(date),
    temp_avg_hour = rowMeans(select(., starts_with("t_ws")), na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  group_by(date) %>%
  summarise(daily_temp = mean(temp_avg_hour, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

head(daily_temp)
## # A tibble: 6 × 2
##   date       daily_temp
##   <date>          <dbl>
## 1 2005-01-01       53.6
## 2 2005-01-02       53.8
## 3 2005-01-03       55.9
## 4 2005-01-04       61.7
## 5 2005-01-05       60.4
## 6 2005-01-06       62.0
str(daily_temp)
## tibble [2,373 × 2] (S3: tbl_df/tbl/data.frame)
##  $ date      : Date[1:2373], format: "2005-01-01" "2005-01-02" ...
##  $ daily_temp: num [1:2373] 53.6 53.8 55.9 61.7 60.4 ...
# process humidity
daily_humidity <- humidity %>%
  mutate(
    date = as.Date(date),
    humidity_avg_hour = rowMeans(select(., starts_with("rh_ws")), na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  group_by(date) %>%
  summarise(daily_humidity = mean(humidity_avg_hour, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

head(daily_humidity)
## # A tibble: 6 × 2
##   date       daily_humidity
##   <date>              <dbl>
## 1 2005-01-01           76.7
## 2 2005-01-02           80.5
## 3 2005-01-03           81.2
## 4 2005-01-04           74.8
## 5 2005-01-05           76.1
## 6 2005-01-06           78.0
str(daily_humidity)
## tibble [2,372 × 2] (S3: tbl_df/tbl/data.frame)
##  $ date          : Date[1:2372], format: "2005-01-01" "2005-01-02" ...
##  $ daily_humidity: num [1:2372] 76.7 80.5 81.2 74.8 76.1 ...
# check NA in temp
sum(is.na(daily_temp$date))
## [1] 1
# inspect problematic rows
daily_temp %>%
  filter(is.na(date))
## # A tibble: 1 × 2
##   date   daily_temp
##   <date>      <dbl>
## 1 NA           71.8
daily_temp <- daily_temp %>%
  filter(!is.na(date))

sum(is.na(daily_temp$date))
## [1] 0
range(daily_temp$date)
## [1] "2005-01-01" "2011-06-30"
dim(daily_temp)
## [1] 2372    2
# merge all datasets
data_all <- daily_load %>%
  left_join(daily_temp, by = "date") %>%
  left_join(daily_humidity, by = "date")

head(data_all)
## # A tibble: 6 × 4
##   date       daily_avg daily_temp daily_humidity
##   <date>         <dbl>      <dbl>          <dbl>
## 1 2005-01-01     3108.       53.6           76.7
## 2 2005-01-02     3068.       53.8           80.5
## 3 2005-01-03     3061.       55.9           81.2
## 4 2005-01-04     2566.       61.7           74.8
## 5 2005-01-05     2496.       60.4           76.1
## 6 2005-01-06     2735.       62.0           78.0
str(data_all)
## tibble [2,372 × 4] (S3: tbl_df/tbl/data.frame)
##  $ date          : Date[1:2372], format: "2005-01-01" "2005-01-02" ...
##  $ daily_avg     : num [1:2372] 3108 3068 3061 2566 2496 ...
##  $ daily_temp    : num [1:2372] 53.6 53.8 55.9 61.7 60.4 ...
##  $ daily_humidity: num [1:2372] 76.7 80.5 81.2 74.8 76.1 ...
colSums(is.na(data_all))
##           date      daily_avg     daily_temp daily_humidity 
##              0              0              0              0

02 exploratory analysis

library(tidyverse)
library(forecast)
## Registered S3 method overwritten by 'quantmod':
##   method            from
##   as.zoo.data.frame zoo
# load processed data
source("Scripts/01_read_all_data.R")

## tibble [2,372 × 2] (S3: tbl_df/tbl/data.frame)
##  $ date     : Date[1:2372], format: "2005-01-01" "2005-01-02" ...
##  $ daily_avg: num [1:2372] 3108 3068 3061 2566 2496 ...
## tibble [2,373 × 2] (S3: tbl_df/tbl/data.frame)
##  $ date      : Date[1:2373], format: "2005-01-01" "2005-01-02" ...
##  $ daily_temp: num [1:2373] 53.6 53.8 55.9 61.7 60.4 ...
## tibble [2,372 × 2] (S3: tbl_df/tbl/data.frame)
##  $ date          : Date[1:2372], format: "2005-01-01" "2005-01-02" ...
##  $ daily_humidity: num [1:2372] 76.7 80.5 81.2 74.8 76.1 ...
## tibble [2,372 × 4] (S3: tbl_df/tbl/data.frame)
##  $ date          : Date[1:2372], format: "2005-01-01" "2005-01-02" ...
##  $ daily_avg     : num [1:2372] 3108 3068 3061 2566 2496 ...
##  $ daily_temp    : num [1:2372] 53.6 53.8 55.9 61.7 60.4 ...
##  $ daily_humidity: num [1:2372] 76.7 80.5 81.2 74.8 76.1 ...
# split data based on instructor prompt
train_data <- data_all %>%
  filter(date >= as.Date("2005-01-01") &
           date <= as.Date("2010-06-30"))

valid_data <- data_all %>%
  filter(date >= as.Date("2010-07-01") &
           date <= as.Date("2010-07-31"))
# create msts object for training data
ts_train_msts <- msts(
  train_data$daily_avg,
  seasonal.periods = c(7, 365)
)
# validation series only for plotting
ts_valid_msts <- msts(
  valid_data$daily_avg,
  seasonal.periods = c(7, 365)
)

# quick plot of training series
autoplot(ts_train_msts) +
  ggtitle("Training Daily Electricity Load")

# baseline model: TBATS
fit_tbats <- tbats(ts_train_msts)

# forecast 31 days for validation period
fc_tbats <- forecast(fit_tbats, h = 31)

# plot forecast vs actual validation data
autoplot(fc_tbats) +
  autolayer(ts_valid_msts, series = "Actual") +
  ggtitle("TBATS Forecast vs Actuals")

# accuracy on validation set
accuracy(as.numeric(fc_tbats$mean), valid_data$daily_avg)
# current MAPE is 11.31%, retrain on all data and generate one sample submission
full_train_data <- data_all %>%
  filter(date >= as.Date("2005-01-01"),
         date <= as.Date("2011-06-30"))

ts_full_msts <- msts(
  full_train_data$daily_avg,
  seasonal.periods = c(7, 365.25)
)

final_fit <- tbats(
  ts_full_msts,
  use.box.cox = NULL,
  use.trend = TRUE,
  use.damped.trend = TRUE
)

final_fc <- forecast(final_fit, h = 31)
# build submission file

submission <- data.frame(
  date = seq(as.Date("2011-07-01"), as.Date("2011-07-31"), by = "day"),
  load = as.numeric(final_fc$mean)
)

write.csv(
  submission,
  file = "Output/submission_basic_tbats.csv",
  row.names = FALSE,
  quote = TRUE
)

03 basic models

In this chunk, we test basic models of TBATS,ETS and Dynamic harmonic regression with ARIMA errors using Fourier terms for weekly + yearly seasonality

# 2) Split data exactly as requested
train_data <- data_all %>%
  filter(date >= as.Date("2005-01-01"),
         date <= as.Date("2010-06-30"))

valid_data <- data_all %>%
  filter(date >= as.Date("2010-07-01"),
         date <= as.Date("2010-07-31"))

full_train_data <- data_all %>%
  filter(date >= as.Date("2005-01-01"),
         date <= as.Date("2011-06-30"))
# 3) Create time series objects
# Multiple seasonality: weekly + annual
ts_train_msts <- msts(
  train_data$daily_avg,
  seasonal.periods = c(7, 365.25)
)

ts_valid_msts <- msts(
  valid_data$daily_avg,
  seasonal.periods = c(7, 365.25)
)

ts_full_msts <- msts(
  full_train_data$daily_avg,
  seasonal.periods = c(7, 365.25)
)

# quick plot
autoplot(ts_train_msts) +
  ggtitle("Training Daily Electricity Load")

# 4) Validation metric helper, only mape is required but rmse created for reference
rmse <- function(actual, predicted) {
  sqrt(mean((actual - predicted)^2, na.rm = TRUE))
}

mape <- function(actual, predicted) {
  mean(abs((actual - predicted) / actual), na.rm = TRUE) * 100
}
# 5) Model 1: TBATS (tuned)
fit_tbats <- tbats(
  ts_train_msts,
  use.box.cox = NULL,
  use.trend = TRUE,
  use.damped.trend = TRUE,
  seasonal.periods = c(7, 365.25)
)

fc_tbats <- forecast(fit_tbats, h = 31)
# 6) Model 2: ETS+STL
fit_stlf <- stlf(
  ts_train_msts,
  method = "ets",
  lambda = "auto",
  biasadj = TRUE,
  s.window = "periodic"
)

fc_stlf <- forecast(fit_stlf, h = 31)
# 7) Model 3: ARIMA + Fourier
#    Tune Fourier terms using validation RMSE
best_dhr <- list(
  rmse = Inf,
  K_week = NA,
  K_year = NA,
  fit = NULL,
  fc = NULL
)
# search grid; keep it moderate so runtime stays reasonable
for (K_week in 1:2) {
  for (K_year in 3:6) {
    xreg_train <- fourier(ts_train_msts, K = c(K_week, K_year))
    xreg_valid <- fourier(ts_train_msts, K = c(K_week, K_year), h = 31)

    fit_try <- auto.arima(
      ts_train_msts,
      xreg = xreg_train,
      seasonal = FALSE,          # seasonality handled by Fourier terms
      stepwise = TRUE,
      approximation = TRUE,
      lambda = "auto",
      biasadj = TRUE
    )

    fc_try <- forecast(fit_try, xreg = xreg_valid, h = 31)

    rmse_try <- rmse(valid_data$daily_avg, as.numeric(fc_try$mean))

    if (rmse_try < best_dhr$rmse) {
      best_dhr$rmse <- rmse_try
      best_dhr$K_week <- K_week
      best_dhr$K_year <- K_year
      best_dhr$fit <- fit_try
      best_dhr$fc <- fc_try
    }
  }
}
# 8) Compare validation performance
results <- tibble(
  model = c("TBATS", "ETS+STL", "ARIMA+Fourier"),
  RMSE = c(
    rmse(valid_data$daily_avg, as.numeric(fc_tbats$mean)),
    rmse(valid_data$daily_avg, as.numeric(fc_stlf$mean)),
    rmse(valid_data$daily_avg, as.numeric(best_dhr$fc$mean))
  ),
  MAPE = c(
    mape(valid_data$daily_avg, as.numeric(fc_tbats$mean)),
    mape(valid_data$daily_avg, as.numeric(fc_stlf$mean)),
    mape(valid_data$daily_avg, as.numeric(best_dhr$fc$mean))
  )
) %>%
  arrange(RMSE)

print(results)

# plot best validation forecast
best_model_name <- results$model[1]
# ARIMA + Fourier is performing the best
  final_xreg_train <- fourier(
    ts_full_msts,
    K = c(best_dhr$K_week, best_dhr$K_year)
  )

  final_xreg_future <- fourier(
    ts_full_msts,
    K = c(best_dhr$K_week, best_dhr$K_year),
    h = h_final
  )

  final_fit <- auto.arima(
    ts_full_msts,
    xreg = final_xreg_train,
    seasonal = FALSE,
    stepwise = FALSE,
    approximation = FALSE,
    lambda = "auto",
    biasadj = TRUE
  )

final_fc <- forecast(final_fit, xreg = final_xreg_future, h = 31)
# build submission file
submission <- data.frame(
  date = seq(as.Date("2011-07-01"), as.Date("2011-07-31"), by = "day"),
  load = as.numeric(final_fc$mean)
)

write.csv(
  submission,
  file = here("Output", "submission_arima_fourier.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE,
  quote = TRUE
)

04 adding external

# Scale Xreg
xreg_ext_train <- scale(train_data %>%
  select(daily_temp, daily_humidity))

xreg_ext_valid <- scale(valid_data %>%
  select(daily_temp, daily_humidity))
for (K_week in 1:2) {
  for (K_year in 4:5) {

    fourier_train <- fourier(ts_train_msts, K = c(K_week, K_year))
    fourier_valid <- fourier(ts_train_msts, K = c(K_week, K_year), h = 31)

    # combine Fourier + external regressors
    xreg_train <- cbind(fourier_train, xreg_ext_train)
    xreg_valid <- cbind(fourier_valid, xreg_ext_valid)

    fit_try <- auto.arima(
      ts_train_msts,
      xreg = xreg_train,
      seasonal = FALSE,
      stepwise = TRUE,
      approximation = TRUE,
      lambda = "auto",
      biasadj = TRUE
    )

    fc_try <- forecast(fit_try, xreg = xreg_valid, h = 31)

    rmse_try <- rmse(valid_data$daily_avg, as.numeric(fc_try$mean))

    if (rmse_try < best_dhr$rmse) {
      best_dhr$rmse <- rmse_try
      best_dhr$K_week <- K_week
      best_dhr$K_year <- K_year
      best_dhr$fit <- fit_try
      best_dhr$fc <- fc_try
    }
  }
}
xreg_ext_full <- full_train_data %>%
  select(daily_temp, daily_humidity) %>%
  as.matrix()
final_xreg_train <- cbind(
  fourier(
    ts_full_msts,
    K = c(best_dhr$K_week, best_dhr$K_year)
  ),
  xreg_ext_full
)

final_xreg_future <- cbind(
  fourier(
    ts_full_msts,
    K = c(best_dhr$K_week, best_dhr$K_year),
    h = 31
  ),
  xreg_ext_full
)

final_fit <- auto.arima(
  ts_full_msts,
  xreg = final_xreg_train,
  seasonal = FALSE,
  stepwise = FALSE,
  approximation = FALSE,
  lambda = "auto",
  biasadj = TRUE
)

final_fc <- forecast(final_fit, xreg = final_xreg_future, h = 31)

05 model comparison

Model Score / Status Notes
ARIMA + Fourier Kaggle 0.0960 Best submitted model
Basic TBATS Kaggle 0.0980 Baseline benchmark
ETS + STL Validation tested Seasonal benchmark
ARIMA + Fourier + External Regressors Validation tested Temp + humidity included
Vanilla baseline Instructor benchmark Starting model

06 conclusion

Among the models tested, ARIMA with Fourier terms achieved the best Kaggle public score among submitted models. TBATS also performed competitively as a benchmark model. External regressors such as temperature and humidity were explored during development, but were not included in the final submitted model.

07 AI Usage Statement

AI tools were used to assist with debugging R code, improving workflow efficiency, and editing report presentation. All model selection, implementation, and interpretation were completed by the authors.
